lessons.title: Leave room for the part people remember.

image.alt: Folded paper, glass of water, pencil, and soft shadow

lessons.body: The fastest version is not always the smallest one. A little structure up front can make the creative part feel less fragile later, and it gives the weird, memorable part a better chance to survive.
lessons.credit: Shared by the house / edited for the issue

masthead.headline: A clean read for a busy house.

cards.label: Occasion board
cards.text: One place for dates, people, and tiny reminders, even when the week is quiet.

items.label: People
items.text: Birthdays, anniversaries, and shoutouts stay together instead of competing for card space.

items.label: Rooms
items.text: Review windows and travel notes get one short line before they become surprises.

items.label: Moments
items.text: The best tiny wins from the week get saved before they vanish into Slack.

items.label: Next
items.text: Anything that needs a nudge lands here until it becomes a full note.

feature.title: Audrey is making the house easier to catch up on.
feature.body: The staff feature can be filled with a headshot, a project still, and a few images from the moment being highlighted, so each issue feels personal without creating a custom layout every week.

businessUnits.title: The house note starts with Summit House.
businessUnits.lead: One big house signal, then the agency lines.
businessUnits.note: This keeps the SH brand primary while still letting each business unit show up with a clean weekly pulse.

supportingImage.alt: Layered paper and blueprint editorial still life

notes.text: Summit House is becoming the front door for the weekly rhythm.
Campaign review is tightening around the clearest story.
The next shoot plan is simpler, sharper, and easier to hand off.
Effects language is settling into something people can actually use.
The handoff layer is getting quieter, which usually means it is working.
The social energy is bright, useful, and worth pulling into the house read.

sharing.title: Public signals worth bringing inside.
sharing.body: A weekly pull from the posts, screenshots, launches, and small public signals worth bringing back into the room.

posts.title: A studio update is only useful if people can find it again.

posts.title: The best campaign notes tend to sound obvious once they are finally clear.

posts.title: A good effect should feel like a point of view, not a dropdown.

posts.title: The post that feels lightweight can still be the one people remember.
posts.caption: Social note
